title: Access variable which is not available in the current scope
description: Access variable which is not available in the current scopeAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugThe variable is not available in the current scope. It will be evaluated to an empty value.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoThe variables defined byARGinstruction have a scope from the definition to the end of the build stage where it was defined. If it was defined in the beginning of the Dockerfile (outside of any build stage), then its scope is restricted to onlyFROMinstructions.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
ARG VERSION=1.0
FROM ubuntu
RUN echo $VERSION

FROM ubuntu
ARG APP_NAME=myapp
FROM alpine
RUN echo $APP_NAME
compliant:
FROM ubuntu
ARG APP_NAME=myapp
RUN echo $APP_NAME

logic.description: This rule uses a custom function to track ARG variable scope across Dockerfile stages. ARG variables have complex scoping rules: (1) ARGs before first FROM are global and only available in FROM instructions, (2) ARGs after FROM are scoped to their build stage, (3) ARGs must be re-declared in each stage where they're used. The custom function implements stateful tracking of ARG declarations and usage to detect out-of-scope access.
